Technical details.Type: BiPAP with TVAPS (target-volume assured pressure support)
Technical details.Modes: CPAP, APAP, S, Auto S, S/T, T, TVAPS

faqs.q: What modes does the HM-BV-30 support?
faqs.a: CPAP, APAP, S, Auto S, S/T, T, and TVAPS. That is the full clinical mode set for BiPAP and home non-invasive ventilation — covers obstructive sleep apnea (CPAP / APAP), mixed apnea with central events (S/T, T with back-up rate), and volume-targeted therapy for obesity-hypoventilation and neuromuscular disease (TVAPS).

Target-Volume Assured Pressure Support — the machine auto-adjusts inspiratory pressure to deliver a clinician-set tidal volume despite changes in airway resistance or patient effort. Equivalent to ResMed's iVAPS and Philips' AVAPS. Clinically important for obesity-hypoventilation syndrome, progressive neuromuscular disease (Duchenne, ALS), and other conditions where volume delivery must be guaranteed.
The published specification is <30 dB — matching the HM-CV-20 CPAP sibling and competitive with the BMC G3 B30VT (26 dB) and ResMed AirCurve class.
4–30 cmH₂O — the full BiPAP range, supporting high-pressure-need patients (severe OSA, obesity-hypoventilation on elevated IPAP). The CPAP sibling HM-CV-20 caps at 20 cmH₂O.

description_text: The HM-BV-30 is Home Medix's BiPAP with TVAPS (target-volume assured pressure support), covering the full clinical mode set — CPAP, APAP, S, Auto S, S/T, T, and TVAPS — across a 4–30 cmH₂O pressure range. Built on the patented SAF algorithm for leak-compensated synchronization. Multi-level respiratory event detection includes flow limitations, multi-frequency snoring, multi-severity hypopnea, and OSA/CSA apnea. Published specs: <30 dB sound, 1.45 kg with integrated heated humidifier (0–5 levels), 253 × 168 × 121 mm footprint. ISO 9001 + ISO 13485 + CDSCO approved. Positioned against the BMC G3 B30VT, ResMed AirCurve ST, Philips DreamStation BiPAP, and Lowenstein Prisma family.
